symptoms.symptom_name: Acute psychosocial stressor (sudden redundancy) immediately preceding onset

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Transient (adjustment) insomnia from an acute stressor
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clear, identifiable acute stressor with immediate temporal linkage is the defining feature of situational/adjustment insomnia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Combined initiation and maintenance insomnia with near-total sleep loss over 48 hours and daytime fatigue, poor concentration and irritability

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Transient (adjustment) insomnia from an acute stressor
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The core insomnia phenotype with functional daytime consequences confirms clinically significant insomnia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Distress and exhaustion from the sleep loss - the patient does NOT feel energised or refreshed

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: In mania the reduced need for sleep is accompanied by preserved or heightened energy and elevated mood; distress and exhaustion argue strongly against a manic episode.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Transient (adjustment) insomnia from an acute stressor
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Desperation to sleep is typical of primary/situational insomnia rather than a mood-elevated state.

symptoms.symptom_name: No suicidal ideation and feels able to keep himself safe on direct, sensitive screening

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Transient (adjustment) insomnia from an acute stressor
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative self-harm screen does not change the diagnosis but is essential to establish the safe baseline tier; it must be actively elicited, not assumed.

symptoms.symptom_name: No prior sleep disorder or manic history and no excessive caffeine/stimulant use

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of stimulant/caffeine excess removes a common reversible cause.
No prior high/manic episodes lowers the prior probability of a bipolar presentation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Over-the-counter valerian and chamomile tried with no effect

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Transient (adjustment) insomnia from an acute stressor
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Documents self-management already attempted; failure of OTC agents does not itself indicate severity or an organic cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Poor sleep-environment behaviours - screen use in bed at night and a cluttered bedroom

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Transient (adjustment) insomnia from an acute stressor
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Blue-light exposure and stimulating activity in bed perpetuate hyperarousal and are direct targets for behavioural (stimulus-control and sleep-hygiene) management.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal neurological examination and a mental state examination showing no psychosis or mania (oriented, mildly pressured speech from anxiety only)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clinician MSE finding no grandiosity, flight of ideas or psychosis supports a benign picture - a sealed in-person finding the AI Doctor can only approximate through history.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals: BP 138/88, HR 92, afebrile, RR 16, SpO2 100%

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Transient (adjustment) insomnia from an acute stressor
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mildly elevated BP and heart rate are consistent with acute stress/anxiety and are non-specific; a sealed clinician measurement not obtainable via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Thyroid function tests and basic metabolic screen (to exclude an organic cause)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal thyroid/metabolic screening would exclude an organic driver, but it is NOT acutely indicated given the clear stressor - reserved for persistent symptoms; over-ordering it tonight is unnecessary.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Thyroid function tests and basic metabolic screen
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not acutely indicated - the presentation is clearly stress-precipitated. Reserve for non-urgent GP follow-up only if the insomnia persists beyond the expected few days to two weeks or other organic features emerge.
